channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Plan to fire artillery over a California highway during JD Vance base visit irks governor
item.description: A plan to fire live artillery shells over a major Southern California highway as part of a military showcase attended by Vice President JD Vance has drawn strong objections from Gov. Gavin Newsom, who said safety concerns forced him to close a 17-mile (27 kilometers) portion of the interstate.
item.dc:creator: SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Palestinians, Israel disagree on whether Gaza's crucial Rafah crossing will reopen Monday
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt will reopen Monday for people returning to Gaza, the Palestinian embassy in Egypt said Saturday, but the territory's sole gateway to the outside world will remain closed to people trying to leave.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: House committee releases new Epstein documents, including ex-Trump secretary interview
item.description: (The Hill) -- The House Oversight Committee on Friday published fresh files connected to the late Jeffrey Epstein’s criminal dealings, including the transcript of a previous interview with former Labor Secretary Alex Acosta, who served under President Trump during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Legally blind Ohio artist says she can't drive, but she can tattoo
item.description: Nicole Morrone was born legally blind, but she still creates detailed, colorful artwork -- on her clients' skin.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, KREX 5

item.title: Live updates as 'No Kings’ protests underway
item.description: The rallies are taking place during the government shutdown as well federal troop deployments across the country. Follow this story for live updates.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: After Charlie Kirk’s assassination, left-wing professors double down on shredding his legacy
item.description: College faculty members attack Charlie Kirk's legacy after his assassination, making unsubstantiated claims about violence stemming from his conservative activism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RECALL: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA allows Boeing to increase 737 Max production nearly two years after door plug flew off plane
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said Friday it will allow Boeing to produce more 737 Max airplanes by increasing the monthly limit that it imposed after a door plug blew off an Alaska Airlines jet that the company built.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of costumed canines get their chance to trick-or-treating at Howloween event in Michigan
item.description: Dogs dressed up as everything from Elvis Presley to Scooby Doo’s Mystery Machine went trick-or-treating in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Mike Householder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mother alleges son was sexually assaulted on Sallisaw school grounds
item.description: "I dropped my son off Tuesday morning thinking, you know, he's going to be safe," Beth said. "His whole world is just changed in a matter of 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Phelan, NewsNation

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees, months after approving the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA staff sound the alarm on disaster preparedness at rally in front of agency headquarters
item.description: Current and former staff of the Federal Emergency Management Agency demonstrated against workforce and program cuts during a “FEMA Solidarity Rally” on Friday, a potentially risky act of protest because some of the same staffers were placed on leave after signing a public dissent letter in August.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City’s mayoral candidates have duked it out in their first general election debate, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city’s education system.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Arkansas boy with terminal illness finds strength in show ring
item.description: Ledger Lamkin started showing animals after watching his sisters compete and quickly found his passion.
item.dc:creator: Caden Michaels, NewsNation

item.title: Utah volunteer heads to Alaska after storm devastates coastal villages
item.description: A volunteer with American Red Cross of Utah is heading to Anchorage, Alaska, to support victims of Typhoon Halong, which caused severe flooding and displaced thousands along Alaska's western coastline, according to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: US military seized survivors after Caribbean drone strike on suspected drug smuggling boat
item.description: The Trump administration conducted a sixth deadly drone strike against suspected drug vessel, killing at least one and capturing survivors in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina schedules execution for man who taunted police with message in victim's blood
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate who killed a man, burned his eyes with cigarettes and then painted “catch me if u can” on the wall with the victim's blood more than 20 years ago has been scheduled to be executed next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP, Dems spar over shutdown, violence, ICE arrests in town hall
item.description: NewsNation brought together members of the Trump administration, lawmakers and commentators to address national issues before a live audience.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tariffs hurting New Mexicans, Democrat says
item.description: SANTA TERESA, New Mexico (Border Report) – As a former secretary of the Interior during the Biden administration, Debra Haaland oversaw wildlife agencies and the conservation of public lands.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida AG sues California, Washington over 'sanctuary' policies after deadly U-turn crash
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has filed a federal lawsuit against California and Washington over the states' "sanctuary" polices, which he claims have put others in danger.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: US rejects bid to lease coal from public lands in Utah as sales in western states fall flat
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — U.S. officials rejected a mining company’s bid for more than 6 million tons of coal beneath a national forest in Utah, marking the third proposed coal sale from public lands in the West to fall through this month, the Interior Department disclosed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: State attorneys general sue Trump administration for canceled solar program funding
item.description: The attorneys general of more than a dozen states on Thursday sued the Trump administration over the termination of $7 billion in funding intended for affordable solar energy projects across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PA system takeover at Pennsylvania airport is 'wake-up call,' security expert says
item.description: An incident where an unauthorized user gained access to Harrisburg International Airport's public address system could have been much worse, a security expert said.
item.dc:creator: Harry Murphy, WGN 9

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton indicted over mishandling classified documents
item.description: A federal grand jury indicted former national security adviser John Bolton on charges of transmitting and retaining national defense information, bringing another criminal case against a critic of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former WWE boss Vince McMahon allowed to enter pretrial program in reckless driving case
item.description: STAMFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former WWE CEO Vince McMahon has been allowed to enter a pretrial program to resolve a reckless driving charge stemming from a crash this summer in Connecticut.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smoothie straw leads to arrest in 1984 cold case murder of New York teen
item.description: A straw from a smoothie cup was the evidence that led police in New York to the man they suspect of killing a 16-year-old girl on Long Island in 1984, the Nassau County District Attorney’s Office announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Capitol Police called to investigate US flag with swastika in GOP congressional office
item.description: Rep. Dave Taylor (R-Ohio) on Wednesday condemned a symbol on the wall of his Washington, D.C., office, which was reportedly a swastika in the middle of a U.S. flag.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska airlifting hundreds from storm-devastated coastal villages
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — One of the most significant airlifts in Alaska history was underway Wednesday to move hundreds of people from coastal villages ravaged by high surf and strong winds from the remnants of Typhoon Halong last weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury deliberates in trial of 3 New York corrections officers charged in inmate's fatal beating
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — A jury began deliberating Wednesday in the trial of three former New York state corrections officers charged in the fatal beating of an inmate that was captured on bodycam video.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump shutdown layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday blocked President Trump’s efforts to lay off thousands of federal employees amid the government shutdown, for now. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Dunkin' launches new Halloween-themed menu item
item.description: A new drink, the return of a fan-favorite, and a bucket? You can find them all at Dunkin' starting today.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Millions of taxpayers face Oct. 15 tax deadline, despite government shutdown: IRS
item.description: (NEXSTAR) – An estimated 20 million taxpayers will need to file before this week's deadline, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) warned Tuesday. While most people filed earlier this year, returns are due by midnight on Wednesday for taxpayers who filed an extension for tax year 2024.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Arsonist attacks Pennsylvania governor's residence; suspect pleads guilty
item.description: On April 13, Cody Balmer climbed the fence of the Governor's Residence in Harrisburg, carrying a bag that contained Molotov cocktails and a sledgehammer.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WGN 9

item.title: Some Indigenous Peoples Day events strike conciliatory tone amid Trump's focus on Columbus
item.description: From Seattle to Baltimore, many Americans were celebrating Monday as Indigenous Peoples Day, determined to see it as a triumph of perseverance over centuries marked by trauma.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4
